content.text: Let me check how the radio options are created and the dialog container styles.

I see the issue. When the system is in dark mode but Gemini is set to light mode, the `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)` styles fire and turn text white, but there are no `.theme-host.light-theme` / `body.light-theme` overrides to correct it. The dialog background has these overrides (line 4669) but the strategy label, radio options, and other text elements don't.

Now I have the full picture. I need to add `.theme-host.light-theme` / `body.light-theme` overrides for all import dialog text elements. Let me add them after the existing dark theme overrides for each element.

content.text:
Fixed. The issue was that the folder import dialog relied on `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)` for dark mode detection, but Gemini uses `.theme-host.dark-theme` / `.theme-host.light-theme` classes. When the OS is in dark mode but Gemini is set to light, the media query fires and turns text white on a light background.

Added `.theme-host.light-theme` / `body.light-theme` and `.theme-host.dark-theme` / `body.dark-theme` overrides for:
- Dialog title
- Strategy label ("导入策略")
- Radio option borders and text ("与现有文件夹合并", "覆盖现有文件夹")
- File button
- File name display
- Secondary button (cancel)
- Primary button (import)
这里中间怎么有一个空的区域是不是可以去掉
[Image: source: /var/folders/fq/d_yn5ybs289gm6d7t2mhgy_w0000gn/T/TemporaryItems/NSIRD_screencaptureui_gPKJZS/Screenshot 2026-03-10 at 23.21.19.png]
That empty area is the `.gv-folder-import-file-name` element — it shows the selected filename but displays as an empty box when no file is chosen. Let me check how it's created.
The `fileName` div is always rendered with empty text. It should be hidden until a file is selected.

content.content: The file /Users/jessezhang/Desktop/Coding/side-projects/gemini-voyager/public/contentStyle.css has been updated successfully.

content.text: Fixed. The `.gv-folder-import-file-name` div is now `display: none` by default and only shows (`display: flex`) via `:not(:empty)` when a filename is actually set. This removes the empty box between the file button and the paste button.

content.content: No matches found

content.text: The icons use `mat-icon` with inline styles. The icon's `font-size: 18px` but no vertical alignment fix. The `mat-icon` class likely has a default line-height that causes misalignment. Let me add `vertical-align` or adjust the inline style.

The issue is in the inline style at line 6935. The `mat-icon` has `font-size: 18px` but needs proper vertical alignment. The menu item's inline `display: flex; align-items: center` (lines 6932-6933) should handle it, but the CSS has `display: block` which gets overridden by inline style. The `mat-icon` likely needs a consistent `line-height`.

input.old_string: menuItem.innerHTML = `<mat-icon role="img" class="mat-icon notranslate google-symbols mat-ligature-font mat-icon-no-color" aria-hidden="true" style="font-size: 18px; margin-right: 8px;">${item.icon}</mat-icon>${item.label}`;
input.new_string: menuItem.innerHTML = `<mat-icon role="img" class="mat-icon notranslate google-symbols mat-ligature-font mat-icon-no-color" aria-hidden="true" style="font-size: 18px; line-height: 1; margin-right: 8px;">${item.icon}</mat-icon>${item.label}`;

fix(folder): fix import dialog light mode colors and menu icon alignment

Add .theme-host.light-theme/.dark-theme overrides for folder import
dialog elements to prevent white text on light background. Hide empty
filename placeholder until file is selected. Fix menu icon vertical
alignment with flex layout and line-height.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
